# UX Data Performance API Security Checklist: Indicate Degraded Forecast Confidence in UI

**Purpose**: Validate the quality of requirements across UX, data, performance, API, and security concerns for UC-16 before implementation work begins.
**Created**: 2026-03-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](/Users/sahmed/Documents/311-forecast-system/specs/016-uc-16-degraded-forecast-confidence/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are the operator-facing display needs defined beyond showing a warning, including what must be visible for degraded, normal, signals-missing, dismissed, error, and render-failed confidence states? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 1 through §User Story 3, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceView]
- [x] CHK002 Are requirements defined for how the confidence-status request aligns to the exact forecast scope and window already being viewed, rather than creating an independent or loosely matched confidence context? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002 through §FR-004, Plan §Summary, Data Model §Reused Shared Entities and Vocabulary]
- [x] CHK003 Are the required attributes of a confidence request fully specified, including scope identifiers, time range, actor identity, and correlation context needed for traceability? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceRequest]
- [x] CHK004 Are requirements defined for all API consumers involved in the flow, including forecast-view readers and clients that report render outcomes, rather than only the operational manager who sees the UI warning? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001 through §FR-015, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK005 Does the specification define what successful behavior means for non-degraded paths such as missing signals, dismissed signals, backend errors, and client render failures instead of focusing only on confirmed degraded warnings? [Gap, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Performance Goals]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK006 Is “clear degraded-confidence indicator” translated into observable UI requirements about message purpose, visibility, and non-blocking behavior rather than left as a subjective design statement? [Clarity, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-008a]
- [x] CHK007 Is “associated confidence or quality signals for the same scope” unambiguous about which scope dimensions and time window must match between forecast data and confidence evidence? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003 through §FR-005a, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Derived Invariants]
- [x] CHK008 Is “materially reduce forecast reliability” precise enough to produce consistent detection outcomes, or does it still depend on unstated implementation judgment beyond the centrally managed rule set? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-005a, Spec §Assumptions, Research §Decision: Apply one centrally managed degraded-confidence rule set across supported scopes]
- [x] CHK009 Is the distinction between missing signals, dismissed signals, normal confidence, and render failure clear enough that the UI and APIs cannot collapse them into the same apparent state? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-010 through §FR-014, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §Canonical UC-16 Vocabulary]
- [x] CHK010 Is “log degraded-confidence detection outcomes and indicator-display outcomes for each forecast-visualization request” defined clearly enough to distinguish backend preparation from final client render reporting? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceAssessmentResult, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceRenderEvent]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK011 Are the non-blocking forecast-display requirements consistent between the user stories, edge cases, functional requirements, and data-model rule that forecast visibility remains independent from indicator visibility? [Consistency, Spec §User Story 1 through §User Story 3, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-014, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceAssessmentResult, Data Model §Derived Invariants]
- [x] CHK012 Are the vocabulary and state transitions consistent across spec, plan, data model, and API contract for `degraded_confirmed`, `normal`, `signals_missing`, `dismissed`, `error`, `display_required`, `not_displayed`, and `render_failed`? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Constitution Check, Data Model §Canonical UC-16 Vocabulary, Contract §components.schemas]
- [x] CHK013 Are assumptions about upstream confidence or quality signals being available consistent with the explicit requirement that the feature must still return a normal display state when those signals are absent? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK014 Are reason-category requirements consistent between the generic user-facing warning language and the data-model or contract vocabulary that includes `missing_inputs`, `shock`, `anomaly`, `quality_condition`, and `unknown`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-008a, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §Reason Category, Contract §ReasonCategory]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the success criteria objectively verify user-facing value, or do they mainly confirm that warnings and logs exist without showing that the operator can interpret the confidence state safely? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §Success Criteria, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK016 Are performance targets measurable for the interactive load path, including confidence-status retrieval during forecast-view loading and render-event acceptance after display attempts? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001 through §SC-005, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK017 Can the traceability requirement be objectively verified without a more explicit minimum set of identifiers or fields that must persist across request, assessment, and render-event records? [Measurability, Spec §FR-015, Spec §SC-005, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceRequest, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceRenderEvent]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are primary UX scenarios defined for both seeing a degraded-confidence warning and safely seeing a normal forecast when degradation is missing, dismissed, or unsupported? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories, Plan §Implementation Notes, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceView]
- [x] CHK019 Are alternate data scenarios specified for partial or conflicting upstream evidence, such as one signal source resolving while another fails or disagrees on materiality? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-004 through §FR-005a, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceSignalResolution]
- [x] CHK020 Are recovery requirements defined for what should happen after an `error` assessment or `render_failed` outcome, or is the lifecycle intentionally limited to observability and fallback display? [Coverage, Recovery Flow, Spec §FR-013 through §FR-015, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK021 Are API error and authorization scenarios covered by written requirements for invalid scope parameters, unknown request identifiers on render reporting, and callers without forecast-view permission? [Coverage, Gap, Spec §FR-001a, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §GET /api/v1/forecast-views/confidence-status, Contract §POST /api/v1/forecast-views/confidence-status/{forecastConfidenceRequestId}/render-events]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK022 Are boundary-case requirements defined for requests that specify overlapping or incomplete scope identifiers, such as a time range without a stable forecast-view or forecast-version reference? [Edge Case, Gap, Contract §GET /api/v1/forecast-views/confidence-status, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceRequest]
- [x] CHK023 Are data and concurrency requirements defined for repeated refreshes, duplicate render-event submissions, or multiple clients reporting outcomes for the same prepared result? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §Relationships]
- [x] CHK024 Are edge cases specified for degraded-confidence assessments where the warning is prepared successfully but the client never reports a terminal render outcome? [Edge Case, Assumption, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceRenderEvent]
- [x] CHK025 Are API pagination, caching, or idempotency boundaries defined where needed for interactive confidence loads and render-event recording under repeated forecast-view usage? [Edge Case, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §API Contract Direction]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [x] CHK026 Are UX requirements defined for how degraded-confidence reasons should be communicated in user-appropriate language without exposing raw model or rule internals? [Non-Functional, UX, Spec §FR-008a, Plan §Implementation Notes]
- [x] CHK027 Are data retention and auditability requirements specified for confidence requests, signal-resolution outcomes, prepared assessment results, and render-event records? [Non-Functional, Data, Gap, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-015, Plan §Technical Context, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK028 Are performance requirements quantified for both the read path and the write path, rather than only stating that confidence should resolve within the interactive forecast-view experience? [Non-Functional, Performance, Gap, Plan §Performance Goals, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK029 Are security requirements specific about role boundaries between users allowed to view forecast-confidence state and clients allowed to submit render outcomes for a referenced request? [Non-Functional, Security, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-001a, Plan §Constitution Check, Contract §security]
- [x] CHK030 Are availability and operational-safety requirements defined for how forecast views behave when upstream confidence-signal providers are degraded, partially available, or temporarily failing? [Non-Functional, Reliability, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK031 Are upstream dependencies on retained forecast lineage, forecast-view context, evaluation or anomaly signals, and weather or storm-mode diagnostics explicitly documented as requirement constraints rather than left as implementation assumptions? [Dependencies, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Constraints, Data Model §Overview]
- [x] CHK032 Is the assumption that one centrally managed rule set governs material degradation validated by linked requirements, or should the spec define minimum governance for changing those rules over time? [Assumption, Spec §FR-005a, Spec §Assumptions, Research §Decision: Apply one centrally managed degraded-confidence rule set across supported scopes]
- [x] CHK033 Are frontend-backend dependency requirements defined for reporting render outcomes, including when a client is expected to post render telemetry and how failures are classified? [Dependency, Gap, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceRenderEvent, Contract §ForecastConfidenceRenderEventRequest]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK034 Is there any conflict between the spec’s requirement to show a normal forecast when degradation cannot be confirmed and the contract’s `error` assessment status, which could still imply a non-normal backend outcome? [Conflict, Spec §FR-010 through §FR-012, Data Model §Assessment Status, Contract §AssessmentStatus]
- [x] CHK035 Is “one normalized confidence-display payload per request” established clearly enough in the requirements, or does it currently depend more on plan and data-model decisions than on explicit spec language? [Ambiguity, Plan §Performance Goals, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceAssessmentResult, Data Model §ForecastConfidenceView]
- [x] CHK036 Are the terms “confidence signal,” “degraded-confidence assessment,” “indicator state,” “view state,” and “render outcome” used consistently enough across the artifacts to support one canonical data and API vocabulary? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §Canonical UC-16 Vocabulary, Contract §components.schemas]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This checklist is intentionally lightweight and author-oriented, with equal coverage across UX, data, performance, API, and security requirement quality.
